content.problemStatement:
Write a program to declare and accept an array / list of NNN space separate integers. 

Calculate the **multiplication** of the 1st and 3rd elements of the array and output the same to the console.

Check the sample input / output below for further clarity.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with an integer N (size of the array) followed by N integers (space or line separated).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the product of the first and third elements of the array.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Array is [10,12,25,9,20]; first element=10, third element=25, product=250.

solution.approach: Only the first and third elements of the array affect the answer. While reading the N numbers we store the value at index 0 and the value at index 2. All other numbers are read and ignored. After reading the whole array we output first * third. This runs in O(N) time per test case and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) overall

code.Python3:
import sys
data = sys.stdin.read().replace('\\n', ' ').split()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
it = iter(data)
T = int(next(it))
out = []
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(next(it))
    first = third = 0
    for i in range(N):
        val = int(next(it))
        if i == 0:
            first = val
        if i == 2:
            third = val
    out.append(str(first * third))
print('\\n'.join(out))
